rulings.Case Title: DAVID HANSON VS. EQUILON ENTERPRISES LLC, DBA SHELL OIL PRODUCTS US et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defendant Equilon Enterprises Llc, Dba Shell Oil Products Us Motion For Summary Judgment On Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 15, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT EQUILON ENTERPRISES LLC, DBA SHELL OIL PRODUCTS US' Motion For Summary Judgment On Plaintiffs Complaint. Denied. Judge Harold Kahn, who is hearing the law and motion calendar for June 15, discloses that a member of his extended family is represented by the law firm representing plaintiff. Judge Kahn does not believe this fact requires his disqualification. Shell Oil's objections 1-4 are overruled and 5-6 are sustained and thus, for purposes of this motion, there is no evidence that Shell Oil refused a request to de-energize the power lines. The court did not consider the evidence submitted with the reply papers. Per the principles set forth in Hooker v. Department of Transportation (2002) 27 Cal. 4th 198, plaintiff presented evidence creating triable issues whether: 1) Shell Oil retained control by reserving to itself the right to approve of the safety aspects of the work performed by Contra Costa Electric through the issuance of a mandatory safety permit and/or by controlling the power lines; 2) Shell Oil exercised its retained control by requiring plaintiff to climb the pole instead of allowing him to use a man lift and/or by not de-energizing the power lines; and 3) plaintiff's climbing the pole instead of using a man lift and/or the failure to de-energize the power lines contributed to plaintiff's injuries. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS VS. JOHN CHIANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 15, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANTS JOHN CHIANG, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS CONTROLLER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND THE OFFICE OF THE STATE CONTROLLER'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The Court takes judicial notice of the fact that the audit that is the subject of Plaintiff's complaint has been completed. Accordingly, the case is rendered moot because the Court is unable to grant Plaintiff any effectual relief. (Wilson & Wilson v. City Council of Redwood City (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 1559, 1574 ("When events render a case moot, the court¿should generally dismiss it").) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SCOTT VS. CLARK EVERETT HAMON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Clark Everett Hamon 'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Tiara Offord And Request Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday June 15, 2015, Line 6, Defendant Clark Everett Hamon's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Tiara Offord And Request Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. The Court is inclined to order that the deposition take place in the Bay Area no later than June 22, 2015 due to the rapidly approaching trial date. Counsel shall appear at the hearing to discuss logistics for the deposition and address the issue of whether sanctions should be awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN LEGARIE VS. MAHMOUD ASSAF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories 7.1 And 9.1 And Request For Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday June 15, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT TALAB QARAJAH'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories 7.1 And 9.1 And Request For Monetary Sanction. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Plaintiff must provide more detail regarding his damages. The Court will allow Plaintiff another 30 days to conduct further investigation needed to provide full and complete responses. Supplemental responses shall be served no later than July 20, 2015 via email. The Court will conduct another hearing on August 10, 2015 to determine whether the supplemental responses are sufficient and address issues about sanctions. Defendant may serve a supplemental brief addressing whether Plaintiff has complied with this Order by July 27, 2015. Plaintiff may respond to Defendant's supplemental brief by August 3, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
